item.text: I’ll use only `orbit tool run orbit.graph.*` shell commands to locate `ToolContext` struct-literal construction sites in production `crates/*/src/**` code, then filter out non-production paths and the defining file. First I’m checking the available graph subcommands so we can query this precisely.

item.aggregated_output:
Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

item.text: I confirmed the runner interface; next I’m listing tool names so we can target the graph query commands directly.

item.text: I hit an environment constraint: `orbit tool show` is failing with `attempt to write a readonly database`. I’m trying the direct `orbit graph` command path next (still shell-only, graph-only) to bypass the write-on-read behavior.

item.text: `orbit tool run` works for graph queries, so I’ll now trace references from the `ToolContext` symbol and inspect only code refs that correspond to struct-literal construction patterns.
I now have all symbol references; next I’m inspecting their graph “pack” source snippets to confirm actual struct-literal construction syntax and exclude mere type usage.

item.text: I’m running a scripted graph-only pass now: get all code references to the `ToolContext` symbol, keep only `crates/*/src/**`, inspect each referenced symbol’s source, and retain files containing struct-literal construction patterns.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc 'refs=$(orbit tool run orbit.graph.refs --input '"'{\"selector\":\"symbol:crates/orbit-tools/src/lib.rs#ToolContext:struct\"}')
selectors="'$(printf '"'%s' \""'$refs" | jq -c '"'[.code_refs[] | select(.file | test(\""'^crates/[''^/]+/src/")) | select(.file != "crates/orbit-tools/src/lib.rs") | .selector]'"')
pack_input="'$(jq -nc --argjson s "$selectors" '"'{selectors:"'$s, summary:false}'"')
pack="'$(orbit tool run orbit.graph.pack --input "$pack_input")
printf '"'%s' \""'$pack" | jq -r '"'.entries[] | select(((.source // \"\") | contains(\"ToolContext {\") or contains(\"..ToolContext::default()\"))) | .selector' | jq -r 'sub(\""'^symbol:";"") | split("#")[0]'"' | sort -u"

/bin/zsh -lc 'refs=$(orbit tool run orbit.graph.refs --input '"'{\"selector\":\"symbol:crates/orbit-tools/src/lib.rs#ToolContext:struct\"}')
selectors="'$(printf '"'%s' \""'$refs" | jq -c '"'[.code_refs[] | select(.file | test(\""'^crates/[''^/]+/src/")) | select(.file != "crates/orbit-tools/src/lib.rs") | .selector]'"')
pack_input="'$(jq -nc --argjson s "$selectors" '"'{selectors:"'$s, summary:false}'"')
pack="'$(orbit tool run orbit.graph.pack --input "$pack_input")
printf '"'%s' \""'$pack" | jq -r '"'.entries[] | select(((.source // \"\") | contains(\"ToolContext {\") or contains(\"..ToolContext::default()\"))) | .selector' | jq -r 'sub(\""'^symbol:";"") | split("#")[0]'"' | sort -u"

item.text: I found intermittent `store error: attempt to write a readonly database` from `orbit tool run` in this read-only sandbox. I’m testing command forms that still return graph data so we can complete this accurately despite that constraint.

The bulk pack output was truncated, so I’m switching to per-symbol `orbit.graph.show` calls in small batches to avoid missing any construction site. I’ll inspect each returned source block for `ToolContext { ... }` or `..ToolContext::default()`.

item.text: I confirmed two construction hits already in `orbit-core`; I’m validating the remaining referenced symbols (including `impl` blocks) to make sure we don’t miss any additional `ToolContext` struct-literal sites.

item.text: I’m doing one final deterministic pass over the full production selector set, with a pattern check per symbol source, to produce the exact deduplicated file list for the answer.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc 'selectors=$(jq -nc '"'[
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/agent_loop.rs#AgentLoop::run:method\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/agent_loop.rs#AgentLoop:impl\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/session.rs#Session::send:method\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/session.rs#Session:impl\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/tool_dispatch.rs#dispatch:function\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-core/src/command/tool.rs#OrbitRuntime::execute_tool_command:method\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-core/src/runtime/engine/runtime_host.rs#OrbitRuntime::run_tool_with_context_and_role:method\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-core/src/runtime/engine/runtime_host.rs#OrbitRuntime:impl\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-core/src/runtime/pipeline.rs#OrbitRuntime::run_tool_with_role:method\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-core/src/runtime/pipeline.rs#OrbitRuntime::run_tool_with_context_and_role:method\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-core/src/runtime/pipeline.rs#OrbitRuntime::run_tool_dry_run:method\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-core/src/runtime/pipeline.rs#OrbitRuntime:impl\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-core/src/runtime/pipeline.rs#resolve_task_id_from_context:function\",
  \"symbol:crates/orbit-core/src/runtime/v2_host.rs#OrbitRuntime::run_deterministic:method\"
]')

printf '%s' \""'$selectors" | jq -r '"'.[]' | while IFS= read -r sel; do
  source_json=''
  for attempt in 1 2 3; do
    input="'$(jq -nc --arg s "$sel" '"'{selector:"'$s}'"')
    out="'$(orbit tool run orbit.graph.show --input "$input")
    has_source=$(printf '"'%s' \""'$out" | jq -r '"'has(\"source\")')
    if [ \""'$has_source" = "true" ]; then
      source_json="$out"
      break
    fi
  done

  if [ -z "$source_json" ]; then
    printf '"'WARN: no source for %s\\n' \""'$sel" >&2
    continue
  fi

  matched=$(printf '"'%s' \""'$source_json" | jq -r '"'(.source | contains(\"ToolContext {\")) or (.source | contains(\"..ToolContext::default()\"))')
  if [ \""'$matched" = "true" ]; then
    printf '"'%s' \""'$source_json" | jq -r '"'.lineage[] | select(startswith(\"file:\")) | sub(\""'^file:"; "")'"'
  fi
done | sort -u"
